# Scorched-Earth Erdős Sweep — Turn 138

This is turn 138. Turns 1 through 137 are consolidated in `turns_history__frontiermath_q2_galois.md` (attached). Re-read it before answering.
Also re-read `mega_prompt.md` and `problem_statement.md`.

CRITICAL — use ONLY the files attached to THIS message. Do NOT open, import, retrieve, or cite any file from your File Library / previously uploaded files, even one named similarly (e.g. another `turns_history…md`). Other unrelated problems use similarly named history files in the same account, and importing one will silently switch you onto the wrong problem. If the attached `turns_history__frontiermath_q2_galois.md` appears missing, do NOT continue from partial context. Reply only with `MISSING_REQUIRED_ATTACHMENT` and name the missing file(s); never substitute a library file.

If this turn drifts into hammering one specific case or sub-instance, step back and ask whether the case is revealing a structural pattern that would close many cases at once. Prefer arguments whose conclusion is a uniform statement.

End this response with ESTABLISHED / GAP / NEXT + tag.
Continue with Turn 138.

## Turn 138 — boundary replacement makes the degree-five tower redundant

The Turn-137 plan advanced to \(D_5/D_6\). Stepping back reveals that no new source-specific invariant can first appear there. Turns 107–132 already reduced every finite marked count to:

1. the full marked maximal pro-\(2\) boundary in the terminal scalar branch; and  
2. first- and second-order simple-module deformation data in every nonterminal branch.

Turns 133–137 have now repaired exactly those two inputs.

The current submission candidate is therefore:

```text
sigma,tau,x0,x1

sigma2 = sigma^40491355905
g0 = sigma2^2
u0 = (x0*tau)^40491355905
u1 = (x1*tau)^40491355905
d0 = u0*x0^-1
z0 = x0^sigma2
c0 = d0^-1*z0^-1*d0*z0
dg = d0^g0
hcomm = dg^-1*d0^-1*dg*d0
h0 = (x0^g0)*x0*dg*d0*d0^2*hcomm

h0*u1^-1*(x1^sigma)*c0
```

The tame relation remains implicit, and \(40491355905\) is the verifier representative of the genuine profinite idempotent \(\omega _2\). 

### Boundary-replacement theorem

Let \(\Gamma_h\) be the profinite object defined by the displayed word and the prescribed \(2\)-core semantics. Put

\[
\Pi_*=
\left\langle \sigma,x_0,x_1\ \middle|\ 
x_0^{\sigma^2}x_0[x_1,\sigma]=1
\right\rangle_{\mathrm{pro}-2}.
\]

Then:

\[
\Gamma_h/W_h\simeq T,\qquad
\Gamma_h(2)\simeq \Pi_*,
\]

where \(W_h=\langle\!\langle x_0,x_1\rangle\!\rangle\) and

\[
T=\langle \sigma,\tau\mid \tau^\sigma=\tau^2\rangle.
\]

The first equality follows because the wild relation is automatic after \(x_0=x_1=1\). The second follows because in every pro-\(2\) quotient,

\[
\tau=1,\quad \sigma_2=\sigma,\quad u_i=x_i,\quad d_0=c_0=1,
\]

so the relation becomes exactly the defining relation of \(\Pi_*\); conversely every finite quotient of \(\Pi_*\) is obtained by setting \(\tau=1\).

Turn 133 established that the local marked maximal pro-\(2\) quotient is also \(\Pi_*\). Hence the corrected common boundary is

\[
\partial_*=
T\times_{C_2}\Pi_*^{\mathrm{el}},
\qquad
\Pi_*^{\mathrm{el}}\simeq C_2^3,
\]

with the fiber product identifying the parity of \(\sigma\). 

Now define \(E_*(Y)\) by the four source-free recurrences of Turn 128, replacing only the obsolete terminal \(\Pi_{\rm old}\)-count by the compatible exact-image count from \(\Pi_*\). Their rank-halving triangularity is unchanged.

I claim that, for every finite \(\partial_*\)-framed marked target \(Y\),

\[
e_{\Gamma_h,\beta}(Y)=E_*(Y)
=e_{G_{\mathbf Q_2},\beta}(Y).
\tag{138.1}
\]

### Why both sources satisfy the same recurrences

**Terminal scalar targets.**  
When every chief factor in the marked kernel is trivial, the target is a tame–\(2\)-group pullback. Its complete exact-image problem is literally a compatible mapping problem from the marked maximal pro-\(2\) quotient. Both sources now have the identical full group \(\Pi_*\), not merely identical low-order jets. Therefore every terminal value, including all proper-image strata and every possible fifth- or higher-order pro-\(2\) quotient, agrees exactly.

**Elementary active blocks.**  
The finite-Heisenberg construction is word-level and applies unchanged to the \(h_0\)-word: in each finite Heisenberg extension one chooses an ordinary integer representative of \(\omega _2\), evaluates the two relators, and obtains a natural duality map of deformation complexes.

Turn 134 proved that on every simple coefficient module the \(h_0\)-word has precisely the old first and mixed second derivatives. Thus:

\[
H^0(V)=H^2(V)=0,\qquad \dim H^1(V)=\dim V
\]

for nontrivial simple \(V\), and the coefficient pairing is induced by

\[
1\quad(T=1),\qquad
1+U+U^{-1}\quad(V^T=0),
\quad U=S^{\omega _2}.
\]

For the trivial simple module, the corrected relation still has initial form

\[
B^2+AC+CA,
\]

so its scalar Hilbert/Bockstein pairing is unchanged and perfect.

Naturality of finite-Heisenberg evaluation gives the same mapping-cone dévissage as Turn 107. Consequently the duality and lift formulas hold for every elementary, possibly nonsemisimple, active module \(M\), not just for its simple head. No fifth-order word data enter this step.

**Central Frattini stage.**  
The target-side active-block collapse, Fourier reconstruction, exact-image subtraction and central-cover transform do not depend on the source word. The only source-sensitive inputs are:

- whether a nonzero filtration-one edge is a perfect affine functional;
- the determinant zero count on the simple head.

The first follows from the perfect pairing above. For the second, Turn 134 gives exactly the old determinant forms

\[
Q_h(c)=
\begin{cases}
q(c),&T=1,\\[2mm]
q(c)+B(c,U^{-1}c),&V^T=0,
\end{cases}
\]

whose zero counts were already matched to the local Kummer forms: minus type in the unramified case and hyperbolic in the ramified case. Shearing, inflated-class descent and the eight-lift central-cover transform are therefore unchanged.

Thus \(\Gamma_h\) and \(G_{\mathbf Q_2}\) satisfy the same terminal conditions and the same four triangular recurrences. Uniqueness of the recursively defined \(E_*\) proves (138.1).

### Why there is no independent degree-five obstruction

Any hypothetical first finite discrepancy has only two possibilities.

If its marked wild kernel has exclusively scalar chief factors, it lies in the terminal branch and is determined by the **entire** common group \(\Pi_*\). Hence a characteristic quotient of \(D_5/D_6\), or of any later Zassenhaus layer, cannot distinguish the two sources.

Otherwise, the target contains a first nontrivial simple chief factor. The active-block theorem replaces the whole nonlinear kernel by an elementary module stage followed by a central elementary Frattini stage. Those stages see only:

- the natural first-order deformation complex;
- the mixed Heisenberg pairing;
- the extraspecial determinant quadratic form;
- smaller exact-image counts.

All of these have now been matched. Higher Zassenhaus coefficients have no separate slot in the recurrence.

Therefore the quartic quotient \(Q_4\) had an important diagnostic role: it proved that the old and corrected marked boundaries differ and that the \(h_0\)-word selects the correct one. Once \(\Pi_*\) is installed as the terminal boundary, climbing to \(Q_5,Q_6,\ldots\) is structurally redundant.

Taking the trivial boundary decoration, summing over tame frames and setting \(L_Y=O_2(Y)\) in (138.1) gives

\[
|\operatorname{Sur}(\Gamma_h,Y)|
=
|\operatorname{Sur}(G_{\mathbf Q_2},Y)|
\]

for every finite \(Y\), within the attached source-free recurrence theorem. The private 5402-group verifier has not been run, so this is a structural proof-stack conclusion rather than a reported machine pass. 

**ESTABLISHED**: Proved the boundary-replacement theorem. Replacing the obsolete marked pro-\(2\) boundary by \(\Pi_*=\langle\sigma,x_0,x_1\mid x_0^{\sigma^2}x_0[x_1,\sigma]\rangle_{\mathrm{pro}-2}\), the \(h_0\)-candidate and \(G_{\mathbf Q_2}\) have identical terminal values and identical source-sensitive inputs to the four rank-halving recurrences. Consequently the Turn-128 source-free exact-image theorem reconnects to the repaired word, and no degree-five or higher characteristic obstruction can be a first discrepancy.

**GAP**: The author’s private 5402-group verifier remains unrun, and the boundary-replacement argument has not received an independent line-by-line manuscript review.

**NEXT**: Freeze the four recurrences with \(\Pi_*\) as the terminal boundary and submit the displayed \(h_0\) block to the verifier. Any failure must now localize to the corrected terminal-boundary identification, the simple finite-Heisenberg calculation, or the determinant sign—not to an unexamined \(D_5/D_6\) case.

[BREAKTHROUGH]
